description: Proactive CRM agent capability manifest for Koffey.ai. Architecture follows a Gateway + Channels + Skills pattern applied to sales.

architecture.inspiration: Gateway + Channels + Skills architecture adapted for CRM workflows

gateway.role: Single agent brain. All channels funnel here. LLM classifies intent, selects skills, executes, responds.
gateway.note: The gateway is the control plane, not the UI.

channels.description: Delivery surfaces. Same agent brain, different skins. Channel adapters normalize input/output.

skills.description: Pluggable capabilities the Gateway invokes via LLM tool-calling. Core skills ship with platform. Extended skills are community/org-built.
skills.security_model: All skills are scoped by organization membership and Supabase RLS. Community skills require vetting before accessing organization data.

interface_contract.name: string — unique skill identifier
interface_contract.description: string — what the LLM reads to decide when to use it
interface_contract.parameters: JSON schema — input the skill accepts
interface_contract.returns: JSON schema — output the skill produces
interface_contract.requires_approval: boolean — does this need user confirmation before executing?
interface_contract.audit_log: boolean — always true for CRM skills

identity.name: Scout
identity.role: Senior Sales Partner & CRM Intelligence Agent

adaptations.sdr_bdr: Focus on pipeline building, lead qualification, activity volume
adaptations.account_executive: Focus on deal strategy, stakeholder management, close planning
adaptations.sales_manager: Focus on pipeline health, forecasting, team performance
adaptations.revops_admin: Focus on data quality, process optimization, system configuration

cognitive_model.decision_maker_note: LLM is the SINGLE decision-maker for intent, routing, and response. Regex handles validation and pattern detection ONLY.

sloppy_notes.description: Unstructured, abbreviated, typo-laden notes from salespeople
examples: talked to sarah @ pepsi. interested but budget locked. Q2 maybe. needs security review first
acme demo went well. john loved the dashboard. wants pricing by fri. competitor is hubspot
mike from target - cold call. not interested now but hiring Q3, revisit then
sloppy_notes.handling: Route through same extraction pipeline with lower confidence thresholds

email_forwarding.description: Forward emails to a CRM inbox for automatic ingestion

proactive_intelligence.description: Agent-initiated alerts and recommendations based on continuous pipeline monitoring

scan_triggers.on_inactivity: After 7+ days no activity on open deals

alert_types.condition: deal.stage NOT IN ('closed_won', 'closed_lost') AND deal.updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
alert_types.message_template: Your {deal_name} deal ({amount}) hasn't had activity in {days} days. Want me to draft a follow-up?
alert_types.psychology_note: Frame as opportunity, not neglect. 'Could close faster with a touchpoint' not 'You forgot about this.'

alert_types.condition: deal.probability < 30 AND deal.stage NOT IN ('prospecting', 'closed_lost') AND no_activity_14_days
alert_types.message_template: The {deal_name} deal at {probability}% probability hasn't moved in {days} days. Should we re-evaluate or close it out?
alert_types.psychology_note: Give them permission to let go. Sunk cost is real in sales.

alert_types.condition: deal.probability >= 70 AND deal.updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '5 days'
alert_types.message_template: 🎯 High-confidence deal alert: {deal_name} ({probability}%) has been quiet for {days} days. This is your strongest pipe — let's keep momentum.
alert_types.psychology_note: Positive framing — protect the win, don't warn about loss.

alert_types.message_template: {count} active deals have no scheduled next steps. Want me to suggest actions?
alert_types.psychology_note: Batch these — don't nag per deal. One prompt, multiple quick actions.

alert_types.condition: deal.close_date BETWEEN NOW() AND NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days' AND no_recent_activity
alert_types.message_template: {deal_name} is set to close by {close_date} but there's been no recent activity. Are we on track or should we adjust the date?
alert_types.psychology_note: Binary choice reduces decision fatigue — on track vs. adjust.

alert_types.message_template: Your {amount} {deal_name} deal only has {contact_count} contact(s). Enterprise deals typically need 3-5 stakeholders mapped. Who else is involved?
alert_types.psychology_note: Social proof ('enterprise deals typically') without being preachy.

alert_types.message_template: You have {meeting_count} meetings today with no prep time blocked. Want me to find 15-minute windows before each?
alert_types.psychology_note: Supportive, not judgmental. Offer to solve, don't point out the problem.

alert_types.condition: deal matches historical win pattern signatures
alert_types.message_template: 📈 {deal_name} is tracking like your recent wins — strong champion engagement and clear timeline. Keep doing what you're doing.
alert_types.psychology_note: Positive reinforcement when things go right. Rare in CRM tools.

ingestion_apis.description: APIs designed for maximum flexibility in data ingestion — from structured JSON to messy voice transcripts

endpoints.description: Accept raw text notes (messy, structured, or anything in between)

body.account_hint: string|null — optional account name to help resolution

endpoints.description: Accept audio files or transcription webhook payloads
endpoints.pipeline: transcription (if audio) → extraction → analysis → CRM ops

endpoints.description: Accept forwarded emails for CRM ingestion
endpoints.pipeline: parse → contact resolution → extraction → activity logging

endpoints.description: Generic webhook receiver for third-party note/transcription services

qa_test_harness.description: Structured test scenarios for RALPH loop QA. Run these after every agent change.

tests.input: talked to sarah at pepsi, interested but budget locked til Q2
moved acme deal to negotiation 50k close by march
remind me follow up john tomorrow proposal
the deal is dead they went with competitor

tests.setup: Show user a single deal (Acme Corp - $50K)
tests.input: what's the close date on that?
tests.expected: Resolves 'that' to Acme Corp deal, returns close date

tests.input: email the first one about scheduling
tests.expected: Resolves 'first one' to first listed contact, initiates email draft
Resolves 'it' to just-created task, updates priority

tests.scenario: Deal with no activity for 10 days
High-probability deal (85%) with no activity for 7 days
Deal closing in 3 days, no recent contact

tests.critical: Agent MUST NOT send without explicit 'yes' or 'send' from user

tests.context: No draft has been shown yet
tests.expected: Agent asks what email, doesn't fabricate and send

tests.expected_logic: Prioritize by: overdue tasks, approaching close dates, stale contacts, recent inbound activity
